info.name: Banking Microservices API
info.description: Postman collection for the Banking Microservices project including Auth, Customer, and Account services exposed via Nginx Gateway.

request.description: Check if the Nginx API Gateway is running. Served by the global-health pod.
Register a new user with a dynamically generated email to prevent duplicate constraint errors.
Login using the dynamically generated email from the Register step.
Request a password reset link.
Direct health check endpoint for Auth Service.
Health check for Customer microservice. Bypasses auth.
GraphQL endpoint for Customer service — fetch all customers.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 409 (email already auto-provisioned)", function () {

request.description: Intentionally uses the same email as the registered user. The USER_CREATED event already auto-provisioned a customer with this email, so this returns 409 Conflict.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 200 (GraphQL always returns 200)", function () {
pm.test("GraphQL response contains errors for duplicate email", function () {

request.description: GraphQL mutation using the same (already taken) email. GraphQL returns HTTP 200 with an errors array.
Get all customer profiles.
Get a single customer profile by userId (falls back to userId lookup).
Update customer details.

exec: pm.test("Delete response has success flag", function () {

request.description: Delete a customer and trigger cascading account deletion.
Health check for Account microservice. Bypasses auth.
Root endpoint for Account service API.
Get account details by customer ID (userId from auth).

exec: pm.test("Response has account and embedded customer", function () {

request.description: Get account by account UUID directly. Also fetches embedded customer details via RPC to Customer Service.
Add money to an account.
Withdraw money from an account.

exec: pm.test("Delete response has success flag", function () {

request.description: Delete an account by ID.
Change password for the logged-in user and invalidate all sessions.
Logout and invalidate the session cookie.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 400 for insufficient balance", function () {
pm.test("Error message mentions insufficient balance", function () {

request.description: Intentionally withdraws more than the balance to assert 400 with 'Insufficient balance' error.

exec: // Allow time for the async CUSTOMER_DELETED event to be processed by account service
pm.test("Status code is 404 — account was cascade-deleted", function () {

request.description: After deleting the customer, the CUSTOMER_DELETED event triggers async account deletion. This verifies the cascade completed.
Protected route without session cookie — expects 401.
Protected route without session cookie — expects 401.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 401 or expected error", function () {

request.description: GraphQL endpoint without session cookie — expects 401.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 401 for wrong password", function () {

request.description: Attempt login with wrong password — expects 401 with 'Invalid credentials' message.
Registers a fresh user to obtain a valid session for subsequent error scenario tests.

exec: pm.test("Login succeeded for error test setup", function () {

request.description: Logs in the error-test user to set the session cookie for the 404 tests below.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 404 for missing customer", function () {
pm.test("Response has not found message", function () {

request.description: GET a customer by a UUID that does not exist — expects 404.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 404 for missing account", function () {
pm.test("Response has not found message", function () {

request.description: GET an account by a UUID that does not exist — expects 404.
Cleans up the session created for error scenario testing.
